title: Té Amo — current thoughts (next-day pivot to pop-up MVP first, brick-and-mortar later)
html:
Té Amo — current thoughts (next-day pivot to pop-up MVP first, brick-and-mortar later)

Source: 2023-12-30 Google Doc. The next-day update to the original Té Amo plan — same author (sister/family-orbit, not Yahir), but reorganized to put the pop-up MVP strategy at the top.

TL;DR

  • The pivot: pop-up at farmers markets / art shows for 2 months FIRST, then brick-and-mortar. Reasons:
    • Raise funds from pop-up profits to save for permanent location.
    • Market research — find out which products sell.
    • Self-marketing — build Instagram presence + word-of-mouth before launch.
    • Network — become known in the target community.
    • Low-risk practice run — if it tanks, quit before major investment.
    • Continuity option — if pop-up goes well, keep doing 1 day/week as ongoing advertising/sales channel even after brick-and-mortar opens.
  • Same content as the previous day — full menu, pricing, financials ($24,800/mo NET goal), gallery model, marketing, locations.
  • The only real change is structural: the pop-up section moved to the top + an explicit "see Final thoughts.." reference link.
  • Net effect: this is the iteration-doc, where the family-orbit author was actively converting strategy from monolithic-launch to MVP-first.

Pattern observed

Two days of consecutive iteration on the same business plan — the first day = ambitious-monolithic; second day = pivoted-to-MVP-first. This is good operator behavior and worth noting because it mirrors Yahir's own iteration pattern with Brotein (ship to TestFlight first), Rankett (free-work clients before paid partner program), and IRL Lore (M1 = capture only, M2 = summaries, M3 = paywall).

The MVP-first instinct may be a learned family pattern — multiple family-orbit ventures (this Té Amo iteration, Yahir's own startups, even the drive thru weed discussions) follow lean-startup grammar. Possibly inherited from college coursework, possibly from Yahir's own influence on his sister, possibly independent convergence.

Open questions

  • Did the pop-up actually run for 2 months? Where (which farmers markets)?
  • Was Yahir involved in the iteration or just observing in his Drive?
  • The linked "Final thoughts" doc — has it been ingested? Worth chasing for the family-orbit graph completeness.

Related

  • 2023 12 29 te amo tea art house — direct predecessor
  • family business orbit
  • mvp first instinct
  • pop up as mvp
  • brotein — same iteration pattern
  • rankett — same free-work-first-then-paid pattern
